goal: Add an isolated Live AICOS network connector that can fetch card payload JSON only after a ready BPK-015 contract, HTTPS endpoint, auth token provider, and injected or default fetcher are present, then immediately route the payload through the BPK-019 intake adapter. Do not mount a route, persist cache, call DB, call GitHub, change cockpit wiring, change runtime execution, or change package dependencies.
scope_out: Do not add a server route, scheduled job, persistent cache, database read/write, GitHub call, runtime execution, cockpit live data binding, package dependency, workflow, or deployment change.
Do not serialize auth token values in returned results, logs, review packets, or docs.
Do not change BPK-015 or BPK-019 semantics.
claims: A live AICOS network read can be represented as an isolated connector without mounting it.
The connector must fail closed unless the BPK-015 contract is ready and liveFetchAllowed is true.
The connector must accept only explicit HTTPS endpoint URLs without embedded credentials.
Fetched payloads must pass through the BPK-019 intake adapter before any accepted cards are returned.
Auth token values must only be used in request headers and must not appear in connector results.
assumptions: The endpoint URL is supplied by a future caller and is separate from the non-secret endpointRef in the BPK-015 contract.
The auth token provider is supplied at call time and is not stored in contract artifacts.
A later block may decide whether this connector feeds a route, cache, or cockpit model source.
dod: The function blocks closed contracts, non-HTTPS endpoints, missing auth tokens, non-2xx HTTP responses, invalid JSON, malformed payloads, and endpointRef/sourceRef mismatch.
The function uses an injected fetcher in tests and does not require live network access for verification.
builder/tests/liveAicosNetworkConnector.test.ts covers success, closed contract, invalid endpoint, missing token, HTTP failure, malformed payload, source mismatch, and no mutation.
review-packets/BPK-022.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
The focused connector test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A route, cache persistence, DB call, GitHub call, runtime execution, cockpit data binding, package change, workflow change, deployment change, or real AICOS credential becomes necessary.
Changing BPK-015 or BPK-019 behavior becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-015 created a readiness contract for live AICOS fetch/cache. BPK-019 routes already delivered AICOS payloads through the card intake. BPK-022 may add the missing isolated network fetch layer but must not mount or persist it.
